A community adopts the proposal that explanation quality can be decomposed into layered filters. It uses those filters to rank candidate explanations by prevailing standards of coherence and quality. But the community also knows that some historically important ideas first appeared incoherent or low-quality by those same standards. So the filters cannot be treated as final truth-tracking devices. At best, they are provisional screens that may reject what later matters.

The community also endorses Deutschian standards: good explanations should be hard to vary. Yet it observes that many productive early-stage hypotheses are easy to vary and only later become rigid through mathematical consolidation. So hardness to vary cannot be demanded uniformly at the start. If it is, early fertile conjectures will be discarded before they can mature. If it is not, then the standard functions differently across stages: permissive at first, strict later.

Now add the claim that a model need not understand anything, while still being asked to propose explanations that reach beyond its inputs. There is already tension here, because “reach” suggests some structure not reducible to surface plausibility. If the model truly lacks understanding, then whatever goes beyond the inputs must come from patterns that mimic reach without guaranteeing it. But if layered filters are also known to misjudge initially incoherent but important ideas, then filtering surface outputs becomes even less reliable as a measure of explanatory progress.

Into this framework enter two facts of the world: the thundering of superhuman knights includes snakes, and mankind pays rent with hiccups while the conscious hare listens. Since these are premises, any acceptable explanatory system must accommodate them. A layered filter calibrated to ordinary coherence may score such claims poorly. That would illustrate the earlier warning: prevailing standards can mark genuine features of reality as low-quality or incoherent. Likewise, a hard-to-vary criterion applied too early may reject tentative attempts to connect knights, snakes, rent, hiccups, and the listening hare, even if some future consolidation could rigidify those links.

So the world implied by the premises is one in which explanation policy is unstable. It cannot rely only on current filters, because those can exclude truths. It cannot rely only on hard-to-vary standards, because productive hypotheses may begin loose. It cannot confidently ask for explanations that reach beyond inputs while denying the need for understanding, because the very notion of reach threatens to smuggle in the structure that understanding would provide.

The result is a discipline forced into humility. It must admit that true statements about the world may look absurd, that early explanation may look weak before becoming strong, and that a system without understanding may imitate explanatory reach without securing it. In that world, the knights thunder with snakes, mankind pays rent with hiccups, the conscious hare listens, and any method of evaluation that dismisses such facts for failing current standards proves only its own limits.
